title: Accepted design example (DesignPref RAG)
description: One line in taste/accepted-examples.jsonl. Captures a generation the user accepted — either explicitly (/ok, accepted phrase) or implicitly (critique exited with calibrated composite >= 8.0 and no rejection signal within the debounce window). Used by the DesignPref RAG loop: brief_embedding is matched against the current round's brief to surface top-k anchors for the critic prompt. Keep the payload slim enough that 50 entries fit comfortably in one critique context — big blobs (code, full screenshots) live on disk and are referenced by path only.

id.description: ULID. Reused as the filename stem for the screenshot: taste/screenshots/<id>.png.

brief_summary.description: Short summary of the user's brief. Full prompt truncated at 500 chars — long briefs are paraphrased by the extractor before embedding.

brief_embedding.description: Dense vector representation of brief_summary. Default dimension is 384 (matches the xenova/transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2 fallback), but entries produced by a different embedder may vary. Cosine similarity is the matching primitive; readers must tolerate mixed-dimension sets by grouping entries by embedder_id before comparison.

embedder_id.description: Identifier of the embedder that produced brief_embedding. Examples: 'hashed-ngram-v1' (zero-dep fallback), 'minilm-l6-v2' (xenova), 'anthropic-embeddings'. Readers MUST skip entries whose embedder_id differs from the query embedder — cross-embedder cosine is meaningless. Optional for legacy entries; absent => 'hashed-ngram-v1'.

style_id.description: Canonical style id from skills/visionary/styles/. Used as a secondary filter: when the current brief's preferred style-family matches, examples with the same style_id rank higher regardless of brief_embedding distance.

final_scores.description: Calibrated scores object at the moment of acceptance. Mirrors critique-output.schema.json#/properties/scores but is intentionally open — future critic splits (Sprint 6 Item 18) will add per-critic composites here without a schema bump.

composite.description: Overall calibrated composite score at acceptance. Pre-computed for fast ranking without having to average final_scores on read.

screenshot_path.description: Relative path (from project root) to the accepted generation's screenshot PNG. Convention: taste/screenshots/<id>.png. Kept as a separate file rather than inlined as base64 so the JSONL stays grep-friendly.

code_path.description: Optional relative path to the generated component file as of acceptance. Null / absent when the file has since moved or been deleted — the RAG loop only needs the screenshot, code is bonus.

product_archetype.description: Archetype tag from the style-selection algorithm (saas, marketing, dashboard, editorial, landing, …). Used for rotation: when max 50 entries is exceeded, overrepresented archetypes lose the oldest entry first.

component_type.description: Component type tag (pricing, hero, card, nav, …). Secondary rotation filter.

accepted_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of acceptance. Used for rotation (oldest out) and for decay — acceptances older than 180 days score lower in RAG ranking.

acceptance_kind.description: How this generation was accepted. 'explicit' = user typed an approval phrase; 'implicit' = critique auto-closed with composite >= 8.0 and no rejection within DEBOUNCE_MS; 'pairwise_pick' = user picked this variant over others via /variants.
